pattern: Root the tree once anywhere, count how many guesses that rooting satisfies, then re-root toward each neighbor in turn by updating only the two guesses whose orientation flips across that edge, instead of recomputing everything from scratch.
hints: Pick any node as a temporary root, build parent pointers with one traversal, and count how many guesses already match that rooting.
Moving the root from a node to one of its neighbors only changes the parent/child relationship along that single edge — every other guess in the tree keeps the same correctness it had before.

So when the root slides from u to its neighbor v, adjust the running count by -1 if [u, v] was a guess and +1 if [v, u] was a guess, propagate that adjusted count to v, and repeat outward over the whole tree to get every node's count in one pass.

edge_inputs.description: path of four nodes, guesses matching one specific rooting exactly
smallest possible tree, single edge, exactly one guess
k is zero, so every node qualifies as a root regardless of guess accuracy
guesses point the wrong direction for every rooting choice, so no root can reach a high threshold
star graph where the center is a natural root candidate
a slightly larger balanced tree with guesses scattered across branches
k equals the total number of guesses, requiring every guess correct simultaneously

stress.description: random tree built via a random parent for each new node, with a random subset of edges (in a random orientation) used as guesses
